# Transcrição: 30-How to Optimize Round #3

## [00:00 - 03:04]

So, during the first and the second rapid-fire round, what we really did there was we validated our personas and our audiences and even our offer. Now, the third rapid-fire round is probably my favorite round, and this is where we actually try to hack essentially the creative side. And again, there's about three tiers to this. So I'm going to take you through an example that we have set up inside of the SAT account, and then I'll show you a little bit more about those tiers. So this is one of the assets that we have running inside of the post-engagement objective campaign. Now, because at this stage, we've already tested our round one, we've tested our audiences and our personas, and now those audiences are being used inside of a conversion objective campaign. What we're doing here with this audience now is this is actually a totally different audience that's similar to the one that won, or if you have a client that has existing website traffic and they may have organic or other things like that, you're running this type of test to either page fans or, for example, people that visited their website 60 days ago, excluding people that visited 30 days ago, because obviously if they visited within 30 days, they're actually inside of your retargeting or bottom of funnel. So those are the kinds of audiences that you're using inside of this round. Now how it works is we basically start off the first round of round three, sorry if that's confusing, where we essentially are testing out the imagery that we're using. So we know the hook, we know the personas, and now we're testing out the image. So we're testing out different versions of that photo. So you'll see here, for example, let's say that this was one of the winning ads that we had in the first two rounds. Well then we try to test out different visuals for that image by pulling through different variations that the client might have, different ads, assets, maybe even a video, for example. If you're using a video, so if it was a video that won as one of the top ads, then what you're testing out here is different thumbnails, testing out if different thumbnails get you a better view, cost per view, for example, and also better cost per conversion. Now the second round of this is where you're actually going to identify the winning image. So in this instance here, for example, we've ran three of the same image only because we only really have three photos from that client of this particular dress. And what we're looking at is the click-through rate here as well as some of the softer stats, the metrics. And you'll see here, out of all three of them, these two had much higher click-through rates,

## [03:04 - 06:05]

and then this one had a much better CPC, and that actually went all the way through to an add to cart as well. So this style, this image here, this visual here, gave us a much better result than the initial image that we ran, which was this one here. So now you can see how split testing, the actual image that you have, can have a fundamental difference ultimately on the end outcomes that you're running. If you really wanted to, you could actually, once you finish the third round of rapid fire testing, so once you really nail down that creative, you could almost recycle that creative and go back and test some of the other personas or audiences that you tried out in your first round that maybe didn't quite hit it off that well to see with a really solid piece of creative that has been thoroughly tested, would it actually perform, would those personas and audiences work well? Now we've got this piece of creative. The second round of this phase of testing is that now we're going to test out the headlines because headlines can have a really big impact. So some of the things that you want to test out here is testing out either the product description as the headline, you want to test out having the hook as the headline, you want to test out either the lifestyle or the feeling of that dress, for example, the perfect summer dress for your little one. So it's more of an arbitrary lifestyle, sort of the feelings angle of that product. And those are really three of the main things that you definitely want to test out the hooks, the actual product description, and then also a lifestyle. Now what I would also recommend is that you test out for all three of those having a headline description, sorry, a link description, some using the price and others using, you know, maybe you're swapping the hook. So maybe you have the lifestyle headline, you know, the perfect summer dress. And then in the link description, you are using the hook of free shipping or whatever your hook might be if you have one available in several colors, afterpay or lay by available, et cetera, et cetera. So this is the second phase is where you go through and you almost repeat this process. You're using the same image, the same copy. We want to just test one variable here and we're using the same audience to see which one is going to get us a really good click through rate and a really cheap CPC. Once we nail that down, then we go into the final round of this testing phase, which is the copy. And here what you're doing is you're testing out long form copy, short form copy and mid form

## [06:05 - 07:25]

copy. And you can also test out different angles of the copy. So you can test out, you know, copy that comes in from with lots of emojis and, you know, bright bubbly buy here. You can test out copy that's, you know, more about the feeling side of things or talking about the the story behind the brand, et cetera, et cetera. So you're using different angles here. And once you go through that phase, again, you're looking at your click through rate and your CPC. And again, you're testing this out on a sort of secondary audience like page fans or people that have are no longer inside of your retargeting sequence funnel. So people that visited the website say 60 days ago. And you're looking for those soft metrics to see which one's going to perform best. Once you've identified that you have a piece of creative that you then pull into your conversion objective campaign with the existing campaign that you're running now. And that should work really, really well. I would recommend you continue running that piece of creative inside of your post engagement objective to build up social proof on it as well. So keep that going until you feel like you have enough social proof and you can start to see some really good stats inside of your conversion objective campaign.
